titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.6

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=17 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:15:11.063798+00 | translation moa=18 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:15:12.974993+00

div.p:
Alexander Dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . EpiscopiS . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Uicecomi˹t’ . Iustic’ . Prepositis . Ministris et˺
Omnibus probis Hominibus tocius terre Sue Clericis et Laicis Salutem . Sciant presentes et ˹futuri nos ad Honorem Dei et˺
Gloroise uirginis Marie . et Sanctissimi Regis EDWardi . et ad exaltationem Sancte Religionis pro Salute nostra et Omnium
Antecessorum et successorum nostrorum . et pro Animabus Illustris Regis Willelmi patris nostri et ERmegard’ Regine ma
tris nostre . et Omnium Antecessorum . et successorum nostrorum quandam Abbatiam Cysterciensis Ordinis fundasse Apud
Balmurinath in fif . nosque monachis ipsius ordinis Ibidem deo Seruientibus . et Seruituris dedisse et Concessisse et Hac Carta
nostra Confirmasse totam terram de Balmurinath . et De Cultran In fif . per Suas Rectas Diuisas . Concessimus etiam eisdem
Ecclesiam matricem de Balmurinath cum Omnibus pertinentiis Suis . et Omnia ad predictas terras iuste pertinentia. Quas
etiam Adam de Stawell’ frater . et HereS Ricardi Reuell’ nobis Ad Opus predicte Domine . E . Regine matris nostre
In Plena Curia nostra Apud forephar quietas calamauit . et In manu nostra pro se et Heredibus Suis per fustum et Bacu
lum Resignauit . Concessimus etiam prefatis monachis Cysterciensis Ordinis Ad fundationem prenominate Abbatie feuth
mure In Anegus per Suas rectas diuisaS . et cum Omnibus iustis pertinentiis Suis . Et nos et Heredes nostri manutenebimus et
Warantizabimus predictis monachis predictas terras cum Omnibus pertinentiis et Libertatibus Suis inperpetuum contra omnes
Homines . Quare uolumus ut predicti monachi omnes predictas terras Habeant . et teneant . De nobis et Successoribus nostris
In Liberam . Puram . Quietam . et perpetuam elemosinam . In terris . et Aquis . In pratis et pascuis . In moris . et maresiis . In stang
nis et molendinis . In uiis et Semitis . et Salinis et piscariis . et Omnibus aliis Aeisiamentis ad predictas terras Iuste pertinentibus .
Ita Libere . et Quiete . Plenarie . et Honorifice . Sicut aliqua elemosina . Liberius . et Quietius . plenius et Honorificentius . Ab auxi
Liis . Excercitibus . Talliagiis . Tolleneis . et Omnibus exactionibus . Consuetudinibus . et Seruiciis Secularibus in Regno Scocie tenetur
aut possidetur . Ita quod nichil Horum Omnino ab eis per totum Regnum Scocie possit exigi preter solas Orationes .
Testibus . A. Episcopo Morauiensi . W. Comite De Buwan Iustic’ Scott’ . W. Olifard Iustic’ Laodon’ . W. filio Alan’
Senescallo . Patric’ filio Comitis Patric’ H. De Baill’ . Th’. De hay . Ioh’. et W. fratribus Suis . Th’ filio Rand’. H. filio . Comitis
Rob’ De hay . Gaufrid’ . W. de Nidin . Clericis nostris Apud Clacmanan . iii. die februarii . Anno Regni nostri xvii.

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, sheriffs, justiciars, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know that, to the honour of God, the Glorious Virgin Mary, and the most holy King Edward, for the promotion of the holy life of faith, for our own salvation and that of all our predecessors and successors, and for the souls of the illustrious King William our father, and of Queen Ermengarde our mother, and of all our predecessors and successors, we have founded an abbey of the Cistercian Order at Balmerino in Fife, and that we have given, conceded, and by this our charter made firm, to the monks of that same Order serving God there now and in the future the whole land of Balmerino, and of Coultra in Fife, by their right marches. We have also conceded to the same the mother church of Balmerino with all its pertinents, and everything that lawfully belongs to the aforesaid lands, which Adam of Stawell, brother and heir of Richard Revel, quitclaimed to us for the benefit of the lady, Queen Ermengarde, our mother, in our full court at Forfar, and resigned them in our hand for himself and his heirs by staff and baton. We have also conceded to the aforesaid monks of the Cistercian Order, for the founding of the aforementioned Abbey, ‘Feuthmure’ [Barry] in Angus by its right marches, and with all its just pertinents. And we and our heirs shall maintain and warrant to the aforesaid monks the aforesaid lands with all their pertinents and liberties in perpetuity against all men. Wherefore we will that the aforesaid monks shall have and hold all the aforesaid lands of us and our successors in free, pure, peaceful, and perpetual alms, in lands, and waters, in meadows and pastures, in moors and marshes, in ponds and mills, in roads and paths, and salt-works and fisheries, and all other easements lawfully belonging to the aforesaid lands, as freely, and quitly, fully, and honourably, as other alms are most freely, and quitly, fully and honourably, from aids, army service, tallage, tolls and all exactions, customs, and secular services in the kingdom of Scotland, held or possessed, such that nothing of these whatever can be required of them throughout the whole kingdom of Scotland except for prayers. As witnesses: Andrew, bishop of Moray; Walter, earl of Buchan, justiciar of the Scots; Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; Walter son of Alan, steward; Patrick, son of Earl Patrick; Henry de Balliol; Thomas de la Haye; John and William, his brothers; Thomas son of Ranulf; Henry son of the earl; Robert de la Haye; Geoffrey, Walter of Nydie, our clerks. At Clackmannan, on the 3rd day of February, in the 17th year of our reign.
